title: invoice — field classification
description: Invoices carry customer PII (billing_address name/phone/email, customer_tax_id) — personal + potentially minor-adjacent (parent guardian on athlete-linked invoices). Revenue amounts are confidential (competitor cross-inference). Payment references (payment_method_id, transaction_id) point at the payment module — no card data. Frozen snapshots (billing_address, currency, totals after finalize) survive as historical records for the invoice's lifetime + archive.

restricted: Invoice.billing_address (PERSONAL — customer name / street / phone / email — GDPR-classified PII)
Invoice.customer_tax_id (PERSONAL + regulated — VAT/GST/EIN; falls under national tax-privacy laws)
Invoice.pdf_url (signed S3 URL — reveals the full invoice on any HTTPS fetch; treated as restricted secret)
regulated_minor: Invoice.customer_id when customer_type='sports::Athlete' AND athlete.age < 18 — under COPPA (US) / GDPR-K (EU minors). billing_address in this case references the parent guardian; the invoice PDF must display the guardian's name (not the athlete's) per legal-guardian invoicing rules
regulated_financial: Invoice.total_cents + subtotal_cents + tax_amount_cents + discount_amount_cents + amount_paid_cents + amount_due_cents (SOX §404 + GAAP + IFRS — 7-year retention, 10 for Enterprise)
Invoice.invoice_number (sequential immutable per GAAP + IFRS + VAT Directive Art. 226)
Invoice.status (state transitions are compliance-critical — every transition audit-logged)
InvoiceLine.total_cents + tax_amount_cents + tax_calculation_id (line-level tax detail — VAT return preparation)
audit_log: Invoice.status (every transition — financial retention 7 years, 10 for Enterprise)
Invoice.total_cents (frozen at finalize; every mutation audit-logged for the ~impossible corner case)
Invoice.billing_address (frozen at capture; every read audited for compliance access review)
Invoice.paid_at (state transition timing — feeds revenue recognition + reconciliation)
InvoiceLine.total_cents (every mutation on a draft-invoice line)
CreditNote.applied_at + applied_to_type + applied_to_id (audit for every credit-note apply)
InvoiceObserver + InvoiceLineObserver + CreditNoteObserver refusals (every refused mutation on frozen fields — feeds anti-tampering signals)

notes: Invoice.billing_address is RESTRICTED (highest classification for PII). The snapshot pattern makes GDPR Art. 17 individual erasure operable via redaction — the invoice row survives (financial retention), the personal fields become { redacted: true }.

Athlete-linked invoices (customer_type='sports::Athlete') require the guardian's PII in billing_address (not the athlete's) per legal-guardian invoicing. The InvoiceObserver validates that customer.guardian is present + fetches guardian.profile for billing_address.

Currency values (subtotal_cents, total_cents, etc.) are CONFIDENTIAL — competitor cross-inference risk. Support-role sees redacted invoice list on the platform surface; unmask requires platform.invoice.pii.view.

PCI-DSS scope: NO PAN / CVV / expiry / cardholder name on any invoice row. Payment method references are tokenised references to the payment module (Stripe payment_method_ids, Paddle payment_method_ids). The mark-paid manual endpoint accepts payment_method IN ('check', 'wire', 'cash', 'other') — no card details.
regulated_financial tagging applies to every monetary field. The retention pipeline honors the 7-year rule (10 for Enterprise).

regulated_minor applies to athlete-invoiced rows — the observer sets a flag on the invoice metadata (`is_minor_customer: true`) that the retention + erasure pipelines use to apply the child-privacy carve-out (COPPA / GDPR-K).
